name: Stafford County Public Schools
districtFact: Stafford County Public Schools serves families along the I-95 and Quantico corridor in Northern Virginia, where many households coordinate school calendars with federal, military, commuter, childcare, and senior-year schedules.

about.title: About Stafford County Public Schools
about.content: Stafford County Public Schools serves students in Stafford County, Virginia, between Northern Virginia and the Fredericksburg region. The district publishes a school year calendar with transition day, first day for all grades, student/staff holidays, staff work days, professional learning days, parent-teacher conference days, grading-period dates, senior dates, high school graduations, and make-up day procedures. For many Stafford households, the calendar is tied to I-95 commute patterns, Quantico-area military schedules, federal work calendars, youth sports, childcare, and senior graduation planning.

about.title: How to Use This Stafford Calendar
about.content: This page summarizes the official Stafford County Public Schools calendar in a searchable format and provides calendar export links. It focuses on districtwide dates; families should still confirm school-specific open houses, graduation details, activities, and emergency schedule changes through Stafford Schools and their assigned campus.

planningTips.title: Planning Tips for Stafford Families
content: Use the transition day separately from the all-grades first day if your student is entering kindergarten, 6th grade, or 9th grade.
Track parent-teacher conference days and staff work days separately from holiday breaks because they create additional student no-school weekdays.
Review Stafford's make-up day procedures before booking travel around February and March, since the district may convert listed non-school days if required instructional time falls short.
Check the official Stafford calendar page for graduation details and any school board amendments before making end-of-year plans.

compareIntro: Compare the Stafford County school calendar with nearby Virginia district calendars currently available on MySchoolDates. Dates shown include the first day, last day, and spring break.

customSections.label: Important Stafford County Dates Parents Should Know
customSections.content: Stafford's 2026-2027 calendar has several dates that matter beyond the standard first day, winter break, and spring break. Families should pay special attention to transition day, staff work days, parent-teacher conferences, potential make-up conversion days, senior dates, and the May graduation window.

groups.label: Parent planning checkpoints
items: Back-to-school: transition day is August 11 for kindergarten, 6th grade, and 9th grade; the first day for all grades is August 12.
Childcare: staff work days, professional learning days, and parent-teacher conference days create single-day student closures outside major break weeks.
Make-up day planning: February 12, February 15, and March 29 may be converted if Stafford needs additional instructional time.
Senior year: the calendar lists May 21 as the last day for seniors, May 24 for senior walks, and May 28-29 for high school graduations.

customSections.label: How We Reviewed This Stafford Calendar
customSections.content: Reviewed by the MySchoolDates Editorial Team. The team checked the Stafford County Public Schools 2026-2027 calendar against the official district calendar page and linked source PDF before publishing this page.

groups.label: Review method
items: Checked the official Stafford Schools calendar page and the published 2026-2027 calendar source.
Cross-checked transition day, first day, last day, breaks, holidays, staff work days, professional learning days, conference days, senior dates, graduation dates, and make-up day notes.
Separated regular holidays and student no-school days from possible make-up conversion dates where Stafford labels them conditionally.
Linked the official source and correction process so families can verify board amendments, weather changes, and graduation details.

districtFaqs.a: Stafford lists a transition day as the first day of school for kindergarten, 6th grade, and 9th grade. The first day for all grades is the following student day.

districtFaqs.q: Can Stafford County change make-up days?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Stafford notes that if the school year falls below required instructional days or hours, the district may convert listed non-school days to school days or add instructional time.

districtFaqs.q: Where should families verify last-minute Stafford calendar changes?
districtFaqs.a: Use the official Stafford County Public Schools calendar page and school-level announcements for final confirmation. MySchoolDates is an independent reference and is not affiliated with Stafford Schools.

districtFaqs.q: How are Stafford County school closing announcements made?
districtFaqs.a: Stafford County Public Schools posts weather, emergency, and school closing updates through official district communication channels and school announcements. Families should use the official Stafford Schools website and district alerts for same-day closure decisions.
